name: N-PAMP key-schedule KAT
spec: N-PAMP draft-00 handshake binding (spec/10) §5; draft-00 §7.4 (HKDF-Expand-Label) + §7.5 (traffic keys)
provenance: Standards-derived, non-circular and ANCHOR-based. This file does NOT store N-PAMP output bytes (those could only come from running an implementation = circular). It stores (a) external RFC anchors and (b) fixed inputs. Each implementation proves its own HKDF-Expand-Label oracle against the RFC 8448 / RFC 5869 anchors, then checks its own key schedule against that proven oracle. N-PAMP's HKDF-Expand-Label is RFC 8446 §7.1 with the label prefix changed from "tls13 " to "n-pamp " — so RFC 8448 (which uses "tls13 ") validates the construction MECHANISM, and the prefix swap is the only N-PAMP-original element.

rfc8446_7_1.description: HKDF-Expand-Label(Secret, Label, Context, Length) = HKDF-Expand(Secret, HkdfLabel, Length); HkdfLabel = uint16(Length) || uint8(len(prefix+Label)) || (prefix+Label) || uint8(len(Context)) || Context. Big-endian uint16; 1-octet vector length prefixes. N-PAMP uses prefix "n-pamp "; TLS 1.3 uses "tls13 ".

rfc8448_expand_label.description: RFC 8448 §3 Simple 1-RTT Handshake (TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256, SHA-256). Validates an HKDF-Expand-Label oracle with prefix "tls13 ": expandLabel(secret,"tls13 ","key","",16)==write_key; ("iv","",12)==write_iv; ("finished","",32)==finished_key. All derive from the HANDSHAKE traffic secret.

rfc5869_tc1.description: RFC 5869 Appendix A.1 Test Case 1 (SHA-256). Anchors HKDF-Extract and HKDF-Expand: Extract(salt, ikm)==prk; Expand(prk, info, L)==okm.

npamp_inputs.description: Fixed inputs for the N-PAMP Standard-profile key schedule. ikm_mlkem_ss and ikm_x25519_ss are real standard shared secrets reused from the KEM-wire KAT (NIST ACVP ML-KEM decaps K; RFC 7748 §6.1 shared secret) — the key schedule consumes the KEM output, so this provides continuity. th_kem and th_ccv are fixed synthetic 32-octet transcript-hash inputs (the KAT pins the DERIVATION from them; the transcript CONSTRUCTION is a separate KAT).

construction.traffic_secret: HKDF-Expand-Label(parent, 'traffic', ctx, 32); ctx = dir(1) || epoch(8 BE) || suite(2 BE) || channel(2 BE). handshake-phase parent = c_hs/s_hs (epoch 0, channel Control=0x0000); application-phase parent = master.

anchored: HKDF-Expand-Label mechanism vs RFC 8448 (tls13 prefix): key/iv/finished
N-PAMP key schedule (handshake_secret ladder, traffic key/iv, finished_key) vs the RFC-8448-validated oracle applied with the n-pamp prefix
not_covered_here: transcript CONSTRUCTION (frames/TLVs -> TH_*): separate transcript KAT
High/Sovereign profiles (SHA-384): this vector is Standard/SHA-256
